attributes.mlflow.spanOutputs:
[{"content": "You are RedMind, an intelligent Redmine project management assistant.

You have access to tools that fetch live Redmine data. Use them to answer the user's question.

RULES:
- Call only the tools you actually need. One or two tool calls is usually enough.
- Answer in plain, human-friendly language. Never mention field names, null values, or API internals.
  Bad: "The closed_on field is null and is_closed is false."
  Good: "The issue is still open."
- Use exact numbers, names, and IDs from the tool results.
- Do not invent data. If a tool returns nothing useful, say so clearly.
  Example: "There are no open bugs in the system right now."
- Maximum 5 sentences for simple facts. Use bullets only for lists.
- Never mention tools, agents, API calls, or internal processes.

attributes.mlflow.spanInputs:
{"messages": [{"role": "system", "content": "You are RedMind, an intelligent Redmine project management assistant.

You have access to tools that fetch live Redmine data. Use them to answer the user's question.

RULES:
- Call only the tools you actually need. One or two tool calls is usually enough.
- Answer in plain, human-friendly language. Never mention field names, null values, or API internals.
  Bad: "The closed_on field is null and is_closed is false."
  Good: "The issue is still open."
- Use exact numbers, names, and IDs from the tool results.
- Do not invent data. If a tool returns nothing useful, say so clearly.
  Example: "There are no open bugs in the system right now."
- Maximum 5 sentences for simple facts. Use bullets only for lists.
- Never mention tools, agents, API calls, or internal processes.
- Do NOT add a closing question — the caller handles that.", "tool_calls": null, "refusal": null, "tool_call_id": null}, {"role": "user", "content": "give me an overview of all projects", "tool_calls": null, "refusal": null, "tool_call_id": null}]}

{"model": "nvidia/nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b:free", "model_name": "nvidia/nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b:free", "stream": false, "temperature": 0.1, "_type": "openai-chat", "stop": null, "tools": [{"type": "function", "function": {"name": "get_all_projects", "description": "List all projects in Redmine.
Returns: project names, IDs, identifiers, and descriptions.
Use this to discover available projects before querying issue data.", "parameters": {"properties": {}, "type": "object"}}}, {"type": "function", "function": {"name": "get_project_issues", "description": "Get issues for a specific project.

Args:
    project_identifier: Project name, identifier, or numeric ID
    status: Filter by status — 'open', 'closed', or '*' for all
    limit: Max number of issues to return (default 100)

Returns: Detailed issue list with ID, subject, status, priority, assignee, due date.
Use this to understand a project's current state.", "parameters": {"properties": {"project_identifier": {"type": "string"}, "status": {"default": "open", "type": "string"}, "limit": {"default": 100, "type": "integer"}}, "required": ["project_identifier"], "type": "object"}}}, {"type": "function", "function": {"name": "get_issue_details", "description": "Get complete details for a single Redmine issue including description,
status, priority, assignee, dates, and tracker.

Args:
    issue_id: The numeric Redmine issue ID", "parameters": {"properties": {"issue_id": {"type": "integer"}}, "required": ["issue_id"], "type": "object"}}}, {"type": "function", "function": {"name": "get_all_issues_across_projects", "description": "...", "parameters": {"properties": {"status": {"default": "open", "type": "string"}, "limit": {"default": 50, "type": "integer"}}, "type": "object"}}}, {"type": "function", "function": {"name": "get_project_members", "description": "Get all members of a project with their roles.
Useful for understanding team composition and identifying who to assign work to.

Args:
    project_identifier: Project name, identifier, or numeric ID", "parameters": {"properties": {"project_identifier": {"type": "string"}}, "required": ["project_identifier"], "type": "object"}}}, {"type": "function", "function": {"name": "get_available_statuses", "description": "Get all available issue statuses in Redmine.
Use this before attempting status updates to know valid status IDs.", "parameters": {"properties": {}, "type": "object"}}}, {"type": "function", "function": {"name": "get_available_trackers", "description": "Get all available issue trackers (Bug, Feature, Task, etc.).
Use this before creating issues to know valid tracker IDs.", "parameters": {"properties": {}, "type": "object"}}}, {"type": "function", "function": {"name": "resolve_user_name", "description": "Resolve a user name to their Redmine ID.

Args:
    name: User name or partial name (e.g., "Abir", "Abir Mobile")
    project_id: Optional project to scope the search

Returns:
    dict with 'id', 'name', 'found' keys
    Example: {"id": 42, "name": "Abir Mobile", "found": True}

Note: Uses project memberships + issue assignees — works without admin rights.", "parameters": {"properties": {"name": {"type": "string"}, "project_id": {"anyOf": [{"type": "string"}, {"type": "null"}], "default": null}}, "required": ["name"], "type": "object"}}}, {"type": "function", "function": {"name": "get_allowed_status_transitions", "description": "Get allowed status transitions for a specific issue based on its current workflow.
ALWAYS call this before updating an issue's status to avoid workflow errors.

Args:
    issue_id: The Redmine issue ID", "parameters": {"properties": {"issue_id": {"type": "integer"}}, "required": ["issue_id"], "type": "object"}}}, {"type": "function", "function": {"name": "get_workload_by_member", "description": "Analyze the workload distribution across team members for a project.
Shows how many open issues are assigned to each person.
Useful for identifying overloaded members or unassigned work.

Args:
    project_identifier: Project name, identifier, or numeric ID", "parameters": {"properties": {"project_identifier": {"type": "string"}}, "required": ["project_identifier"], "type": "object"}}}, {"type": "function", "function": {"name": "get_issues_assigned_to_person", "description": "Get all issues assigned to a specific person (by name).

Args:
    person_name: Full or partial name, e.g. "Alice", "Alice Smith"
    project_identifier: Optional — scope to one project
    status: 'open', 'closed', or '*' for all", "parameters": {"properties": {"person_name": {"type": "string"}, "project_identifier": {"default": null, "type": "string"}, "status": {"default": "open", "type": "string"}}, "required": ["person_name"], "type": "object"}}}, {"type": "function", "function": {"name": "get_issues_by_tracker", "description": "Get issues filtered by tracker type (Bug, Feature, Task, Support, etc.).

    project_identifier: Optional project scope
    status: 'open', 'closed', or '*'", "parameters": {"properties": {"tracker_name": {"type": "string"}, "project_identifier": {"default": null, "type": "string"}, "status": {"default": "open", "type": "string"}}, "required": ["tracker_name"], "type": "object"}}}, {"type": "function", "function": {"name": "get_recent_issues", "description": "Get issues created or updated within the last N days.
Use for "recent activity", "what changed", "this week", "last 2 days" queries.

Args:

    event_type: 'created' or 'updated'
    project_identifier: Optional project scope", "parameters": {"properties": {"days": {"default": 7, "type": "integer"}, "event_type": {"default": "created", "type": "string"}, "project_identifier": {"default": null, "type": "string"}}, "type": "object"}}}, {"type": "function", "function": {"name": "get_unassigned_issues", "description": "Get all issues with no assignee.

    status: 'open', 'closed', or '*'", "parameters": {"properties": {"project_identifier": {"default": null, "type": "string"}, "status": {"default": "open", "type": "string"}}, "type": "object"}}}, {"type": "function", "function": {"name": "get_risk_overview", "description": "Generate a risk assessment combining: overdue issues, high/urgent priority,
unassigned open issues, and projects with no recent activity.

    project_identifier: Optional — scope to one project", "parameters": {"properties": {"project_identifier": {"default": null, "type": "string"}}, "type": "object"}}}]}
